tasks.title: Validate canonical SKU key across ERP APIs
tasks.objective: Resolve q_008 by identifying the canonical SKU key and raw ERP SKU references needed to join SKU, inventory, warehouse, movement, and sales API surfaces.

inScope: Inspect ERP API field inventories supplied for SKU, inventory, warehouse, movement, and sales endpoints.
Produce a SKU identifier mapping artifact with canonical key, raw references, join assumptions, and unresolved mismatches.
Define the minimum canonical key rule required before downstream joins are trusted.
outOfScope: Accept non-API sources to resolve SKU identity.

acceptanceCriteria.description: A q_008 evidence artifact identifies the canonical SKU key and maps every raw ERP SKU reference needed for required v1 metrics.
acceptanceCriteria.measurableOutcome: Each relevant ERP API surface has a listed SKU field, join rule, confidence level, and blocker status.

testPlan.description: Review the SKU mapping artifact against all known ERP API surfaces.
testPlan.expectedRedSignal: Any required API surface lacks a SKU field mapping or the canonical key is ambiguous.
testPlan.expectedGreenSignal: All required API surfaces map to one canonical SKU rule or explicitly documented transform.

implementationNotes: This is the first task because every later join and metric depends on stable SKU identity.
Do not collapse raw ERP identifiers too early; preserve raw references for traceability.

metadata.blocker: Strong Lingxing candidate fields are available, but same-SKU cross-interface payload evidence is still missing, so the canonical join rule and API statistics chain entry point are not yet proven.
inputs: ERP API field descriptions for SKU, inventory, warehouse, movement, and sales endpoints

tasks.title: Validate default local warehouse identifier
tasks.objective: Resolve q_014 by identifying which ERP warehouse representation corresponds to the default local warehouse business concept used by the required warehouse quantity field.

inScope: Inspect ERP warehouse API fields and inventory quantity surfaces.
Identify the ERP warehouse identifier or identifiers that represent local warehouse quantity.
Record whether there is a single local warehouse or multiple candidates requiring later mapping.
outOfScope: Resolve q_018 before q_014 evidence exists.
Use WMS or other systems to define the warehouse source.

acceptanceCriteria.description: A q_014 evidence artifact identifies how default local warehouse is represented in ERP API data and whether multiple local warehouse candidates exist.
acceptanceCriteria.measurableOutcome: The artifact lists ERP warehouse id field, local warehouse selection basis, and whether q_018 mapping work is required.

testPlan.description: Review the warehouse mapping evidence against representative inventory records.
testPlan.expectedRedSignal: Default local warehouse cannot be tied to an ERP API warehouse identifier or selection basis.
testPlan.expectedGreenSignal: Default local warehouse has an explicit ERP identifier or a documented set of candidates for q_018.

implementationNotes: This task follows q_008 so SKU-specific warehouse quantities can be validated against stable SKU identity.
Do not treat a single warehouse as unique unless ERP evidence supports it.

inputs: ERP warehouse and inventory API field descriptions

tasks.title: Freeze ERP source mapping and normalized record contracts
tasks.objective: Turn q_008 and q_014 evidence into a complete ERP source mapping matrix and normalized record contract for SKU references, inventory snapshots, warehouse quantities, movement events, and sales events.

inScope: Define normalized records for SkuMasterRecord, InventorySnapshotRecord, MovementEventRecord, and SalesEventRecord.
Map every required v1 contract input to an ERP API field or endpoint group.
Record provenance fields needed by validation evidence.
outOfScope: Broaden source access beyond ERP APIs.

acceptanceCriteria.description: The source mapping matrix and normalized record contracts cover all required inputs for current inventory, default-local-warehouse quantity, 30-day outbound, 30/60/90-day sales, and turnover days.
acceptanceCriteria.measurableOutcome: No required v1 metric input remains unmapped or untyped at the planning artifact level.

testPlan.description: Check the source mapping matrix against the v1 metric contract.
testPlan.expectedRedSignal: Any required metric input lacks endpoint, field, provenance, or timestamp mapping.
testPlan.expectedGreenSignal: Every required metric input has endpoint, field, provenance, timestamp, and normalized record placement.

implementationNotes: This task produces architecture-level contracts for future implementation; it does not create runtime code.
The source mapping must preserve raw ERP provenance for later derivation traces.

tasks.title: Validate time-dimension ERP API derivation path
tasks.objective: Resolve q_016 by proving whether 30-day outbound and 30/60/90-day sales can be derived from ERP APIs through normalized records and traceable aggregation.

inScope: Select representative SKU samples for time-window validation.
Define derivation trace shape from raw ERP payload to normalized event to daily fact to final metric.
Record whether required time-window metrics are derivable inside the ERP API-only boundary.
outOfScope: Accept export or non-API fallback as default behavior.
Declare metrics complete without representative SKU evidence.

acceptanceCriteria.description: A q_016 derivation evidence package shows raw ERP records, normalized records, daily aggregations, and final 30/60/90-day outputs for representative SKUs.
acceptanceCriteria.measurableOutcome: The evidence demonstrates whether each required time-window metric can be derived from ERP APIs without hidden fallback.

testPlan.description: Review derivation traces for representative SKUs across outbound and sales windows.
testPlan.expectedRedSignal: Any required window lacks traceable raw ERP source data, timestamp rule, or aggregation formula.
testPlan.expectedGreenSignal: Every required window has raw source, normalized event, daily fact, rollup, and final metric trace.

implementationNotes: This is the highest-risk validation task and blocks API contract finalization.
If q_016 fails, the next step is a separate boundary decision; do not silently introduce fallback.

tasks.title: Finalize default_local_wid or explicit local warehouse mapping rule
tasks.objective: Resolve q_018 by deciding whether default_local_wid configuration or an explicit mapping rule is required, based on q_014 warehouse evidence and q_016 validation context.

inScope: Define the local warehouse mapping rule only if multiple local warehouses exist.
Specify mappingMode values needed in the API response.
Record how mapping evidence will be reviewed during implementation.
outOfScope: Treat q_018 as required if q_014 proves there is only one local warehouse.
Use another source system to resolve warehouse semantics.

acceptanceCriteria.description: A q_018 mapping artifact states whether default_local_wid or explicit mapping is required and defines the exact rule if required.
acceptanceCriteria.measurableOutcome: The mapping rule can explain default-local-warehouse quantity selection for both single-local-warehouse and multi-local-warehouse cases.

testPlan.description: Review mapping rule against q_014 warehouse evidence and representative SKU scenarios.
testPlan.expectedRedSignal: Mapping rule assumes natural uniqueness without evidence or cannot handle multiple local warehouses.
testPlan.expectedGreenSignal: Mapping rule is either proven unnecessary or explicitly defines default_local_wid or equivalent mapping.

implementationNotes: This task intentionally follows q_016 in the validation-first sequence, even though it derives from q_014.
The output should be a planning/config contract, not runtime config code.

tasks.title: Freeze V1 API contract and response fixtures
tasks.objective: Define the v1 API contract and representative response fixtures after source identity, warehouse semantics, time-window derivation, and mapping rules are validated.

inScope: Define request shape, response shape, derivation metadata, and error cases for the primary v1 SKU analysis API.
Include current inventory, default-local-warehouse quantity, 30-day outbound, turnover days, and 30/60/90-day sales volume.
Produce response fixtures for normal, zero-sales, ambiguous mapping, and derivation discrepancy cases.
outOfScope: Define optional extra consumer interface without concrete need.

acceptanceCriteria.description: The v1 API contract artifact and response fixtures include all required fields and make mapping and derivation metadata visible.
acceptanceCriteria.measurableOutcome: Contract fixtures demonstrate full metric response shape and required metadata without depending on hidden source paths.

testPlan.description: Review the API contract against requirement and architecture freeze invariants.
testPlan.expectedRedSignal: The contract omits a required metric, hides warehouse mapping, or lacks derivation metadata.
testPlan.expectedGreenSignal: The contract includes all required metrics, mapping metadata, derivation metadata, and boundary-safe error cases.

implementationNotes: The API contract should be frozen only after q_008, q_014, q_016, and q_018 are resolved or explicitly marked unnecessary.
Do not add presentation/export concerns to this contract.

tasks.title: Assemble validation evidence plan and boundary review gate
tasks.objective: Define the validation evidence package and boundary review gate required before implementation can be accepted as aligned with the ERP API-only architecture.

inScope: Define evidence artifacts for source mapping, derivation traces, warehouse mapping, API fixtures, discrepancy reports, and boundary review.
Define pass/fail gates for hidden fallback, source expansion, and incomplete metric contract.
Connect validation evidence to the tasks that produce it.

acceptanceCriteria.description: The validation plan defines evidence requirements and pass/fail gates for every requirement-freeze acceptance criterion.
acceptanceCriteria.measurableOutcome: Every acceptance criterion maps to at least one evidence artifact and one boundary-safe review check.

testPlan.description: Review validation plan coverage against requirement and architecture freeze.
testPlan.expectedRedSignal: Any required metric, boundary invariant, or unresolved question lacks evidence coverage.
testPlan.expectedGreenSignal: All required metrics, boundary invariants, and q_008/q_014/q_016/q_018 validation points have explicit evidence gates.

implementationNotes: This task creates acceptance discipline for implementation without implementing the validation harness.
The validation sidecar must not become an alternate production data path.

tasks.title: Prepare implementation readiness handoff
tasks.objective: Produce a downstream implementation readiness package that summarizes validated inputs, frozen interfaces, dependency order, and remaining execution gates without starting code changes.

inScope: Summarize completed validation artifacts and unresolved blockers.
Confirm which modules are ready for implementation sequencing.
Identify whether implementation can start or must wait on q_008, q_014, q_016, or q_018 outputs.
outOfScope: Reopen requirement or architecture freeze without a material contradiction.

acceptanceCriteria.description: The readiness package states whether implementation may begin, lists validated inputs, and names any remaining blocker by task and question id.
acceptanceCriteria.measurableOutcome: The package gives a clear go/no-go decision for implementation without modifying code.

testPlan.description: Review readiness package against the validation-first dependency chain.
testPlan.expectedRedSignal: Readiness claims implementation can start while required validation artifacts are missing.
testPlan.expectedGreenSignal: Readiness accurately reflects validation completion and blocks implementation if q_008, q_014, q_016, or q_018 gates are incomplete.

implementationNotes: This is the terminal planning task before any implementation thread.
The output should be suitable as a new handoff for implementation, not an implementation itself.
